difficulty_notes: Overlap of the 7-day and 2-day windows drives the exclusion.
Need monthly revenue extremes after picking the top annual source.
First visit and first transaction are separate event dates per visitor.
Requires daily, weekly, and monthly rollups off the same source.
Must follow the home-page path and keep the max dwell time.
Visitor totals are re-aggregated by month and purchase state.
The last event can be a visit or transaction, so the endpoint logic matters.
This is a cohort filter with a delayed purchase check.
Find the busiest assignee first, then rank jurisdictions within that year.
Need nested ranking for assignees, years, and country codes.
Forward citations are constrained by filing-date windows.
One code per patent, then keep only patents where the code repeats 10+ times.
Combines citation windows with a similarity lookup across the same filing year.
Ranking similar patents needs the filing-year constraint preserved.
Top families are chosen before joining back to abstracts.
This mixes distinct list extraction with citation-family relationships.
Originality score is a derived formula over backward-citation IPC counts.
Exponential moving average is computed per CPC group and then compared by year.
Need the best EMA year per CPC group after filtering invalid records.
Filter out DENSO as the citing assignee and group by first CPC subclass.
Section 101 rejections need to be paired with grant and claim-length fields.
Independent-claim ranking depends on earliest-publication deduping per application.
Backward and forward citations use different date anchors.
Both citation windows and CPC filters apply before the final top-1 sort.
Citation counts are bounded around the application date.
The license, file, watcher, issue, and fork filters all need to line up.
README lines must be cleaned before language aggregation.
The Python file condition is nested inside the watch-event ranking.
Clean GitHub URLs after selecting the most downloaded package version.
Need the provider's annual cost profile after identifying the top cumulative spender.
Return the per-point track, not just the final distance ranking.
Yearly top-1000 filtering changes the state ranking before the final rollup.
Boundary containment and sorted hurricane-name lists both need to line up per city and zip.
The score comes from comparing four monthly averages, not just one metric.
Need the best pickup and dropoff zip pair, then break ties by zip order.
Moving averages and lagged differences make this a window-heavy time series task.
Trip-to-neighborhood mapping and weather joins both sit in the main path.
Forecast-date filtering and radius-based weather selection both matter.
Complaint counts are correlated against station temperature series after daily aggregation.
The complete zip-by-hour grid plus lags and moving averages pushes this into the hardest band.
The answer depends on historical features that are no longer present in the current layer.
Need the farthest grid cell after finding each cell's nearest hospital distance.
Mint and burn inputs have to be decoded from hex before the daily rollup.
This is a broad per-address profile with many joins and metrics, not just a single balance query.
Requires derived age bands and a route string from start to end stations.
Order within the stop sequence changes the answer.
Session gap uses the next event in the same session.
June 2019 is base only for the growth calculations.
Track each cohort month across the first four months after purchase.
June 2019 is a base period and is omitted from the final output.
Filters on user signup year and inventory creation year before grouping.
Two ranking passes are needed: states first, then counties within the fourth-ranked state.
Need overlap between two separate top-20 rankings.
Population and income are allocated by overlapping area.
Four separate leaderboard slices are unioned into one table.
Shot coordinates need basket-side adjustment before averaging.
Rank team markets first, then expand to game-level tournament records.
Combines tournament outcomes with pace and efficiency features.
Multiple filters feed four summary metrics and a max-row lookup.
The answer list comes from a referenced document rather than the database alone.
Latest evaluation group per state with adjusted acreage across macroplots and subplots.
Port, storm, and geometry data all need to line up before counting distinct storms.
Compares solar metrics at both postal-code and census-tract levels.
Find the BRCA1 span first, then rank missense variants inside that region.
Percentage over a filtered accident subset.
Builds a labeled dataset with derived speed-difference buckets and a fatality label.
The source of truth is a reference document for the 2015 crash details.
State-level percentage changes are compared to the national average.
Computes a Pearson correlation across yearly birth and census measures.
Matches city measurements by rounded coordinates before ranking the differences.
Split Stack Overflow tags after counting Hacker News references.
Monthly cohorts with chained 30-day follow-up rates.
Each comment or answer has to be tied back to its parent question tags.
Association rules for a question can come from several answer paths.
Builds article vectors from weighted word embeddings and normalizes them.
Same embedding pipeline, then cosine similarity against a query vector.
Derives article vectors and ranks them by similarity to one reference article.
Series size is only valid after excluding linked instances and bad transfer syntaxes.
Unnest specimen-prep sequences before counting code-matching pairs.
Compares two patient cohorts built from different variation metrics.
Geometry checks and exclusion rules gate the final CT series report.
Need monthly percentage normalization before pairwise correlation.
Need overlap-weighted copy number per cytoband before CNV typing.
Need pairwise gene-miRNA correlations before deriving the t-statistic.
Train/test split before nearest-centroid stage assignment.
All three alterations must co-occur in one clone.
Pair construction, publication-date ranking, and UUID generation make this a dense multi-step join.
Preserve embedded numeric commas while normalizing other delimiters.
Need per-topic first-message identification and distinct voter counts before comparing to the overall average.
Need distinct upvotes in both directions from the same vote table.
Requires matching at both line-item and customer aggregate levels.
Use missing-invoice orders, then compare category maxima to the overall mean.
Need to detect a missing month inside each user's active span and report the latest such gap.
Need topic-level rollups under the General parent forum with missing values treated as zero.
Requires comparing upvotes sent and returned between the same two users.
Compare discharge and vehicle codes only for white individuals, then aggregate by age group.
Use EntityAxisConditions to link ICD-10 codes to deaths before splitting by race and cause.
Need to compress repeated page visits while preserving the session order.
Retention windows depend on Shanghai-local dates, not raw timestamps.
Linear regression feeds a centered moving average over future dates.
Need per-match maximum over identification before ranking bowlers globally.
Anagram counting is case-sensitive and grouped by word length and prefix.
Need one winner per league with a tie-break to keep only one team.
Season-level top-3 lists on both batting and bowling sides with matched output positions.
Pair ordering depends on player score ties and can return multiple rows per match.
Salary values must be cleaned before ranking companies inside each city.
Excludes non-bowler dismissals and combines wickets, economy, strike rate, and best-match bowling.
Wide career profile with many derived batting and bowling metrics.
Requires classifying models by step/version before counting affected problems.
Counts L1_model occurrences separately for strong and soft classifications.
Needs full expansion of nested packaging before averaging leaf quantities.
Recursive rollup over packaging hierarchy with a threshold filter on total quantity.
FIFO inventory allocation across order lines with strict quantity matching.
Pick percentage comes from overlap between ordered demand and inventory lots.
FIFO picking is simpler than the other allocation tasks but still needs cumulative lot handling.
Centered moving average and seasonality-adjusted ratio checks across 2017.
Forecasting uses seasonality adjustments plus weighted regression.
Recursive monthly inventory adjustment makes this an iterative state problem.
Summary includes deviation buckets around the average loss rate.
Multi-year category reporting with several financial measures per group.
Seller summary combines performance metrics with a sales threshold filter.
Averages percent change across several attribute types and their values.
Count each session once per page even if the same page is both landing and exit.
Looks for the most common third page after two consecutive detail visits.
Finds sessions with the minimum count of qualifying events before the first target click.
Overtake counting depends on pit stops, retirements, and lap-state transitions.
Same overtake logic as the longer task, but restricted to the first five laps.
Counts seasons where each constructor had the lowest point-scoring driver total.
Ranks years by the sum of driver and constructor peak points from results only.
Combines best driver and best constructor points per year.
Checks constructor continuity between the first and last race within a season.
Averages missed-race gaps only for drivers who switch constructors across the hiatus.
Compares on-track overtakes made versus suffered while excluding pit and start movements.
Second-highest growth rate with a minimum population threshold.
As-of-date ranking over active banks with an uninsured-assets percentage.
As-of counts at two dates drive the percent change by state.
Pairs a financial inflow measure with a seasonally adjusted index change.
Cumulative ratios by date across multiple mortgage stress categories.
Nearest-neighbor search using straight-line distance between store locations.
One-hour window analysis of push notification engagement.
Wide daily report joins sales, traffic, inventory, and net PPM data.
Selects the northernmost addresses inside the largest Florida zip code area.
Side-by-side road length comparison by class and subclass.
Hourly market and load report with timezone alignment and net load calculations.
Block group population ratios are computed against census tract totals.
